Dear Papa
Father's Day · A Letter in Pictures

Every road
led back to you

Shimla's hills, Bangalore's red earth, a hundred small mornings — wherever we went, you were the reason it felt like home. This is for you, Papa.

Scroll the album
Shimla, before the rain
Chapter One

The man who always finds the view

You have a habit, Papa — wherever we travel, you find the spot worth standing in. That square in Shimla, clouds rolling in overhead, the old blue-and-white building behind you like something out of a postcard.

You weren't posing for the photo. You were just taking it all in. And that's the easiest thing in the world to photograph.

"

A father is the one steady thing a child holds onto while learning to let go of everything else.

— for Papa, who never let go
Chapter Two

Still finding reasons to wander

Lights coming on across the glasshouse, the day cooling into evening, and there you both are — still finding new gardens to walk through, still happiest a little ways off the beaten path.

This is what we hope for, honestly. Not grand gestures. Just more evenings exactly like this one, the two of you, side by side.

"

Some men give their children the world. Ours gave us himself, which turned out to be the bigger gift.

— a thing we only understood later
All five of us, one frame
Chapter Three

The whole crew, finally still

Getting all of us in one place, looking at the same camera, at the same time, is genuinely one of the harder logistical feats of our lives. But here we are — lights overhead, everyone actually smiling instead of mid-blink.

This is the photo we'll look back on. Not because the lights were pretty, though they were, but because for one evening, your whole family was simply together, and you were right there in the middle of it, exactly where you belong.

Pages from the album

Every trip, every roadside stop, every "just one more photo," every silly one we almost deleted — they've all added up to this. Tap any one of them.

Golden hour, the whole hillside lit up
Nandi Hills, just before sunset
Mid-walk, never quite still
Lalbagh's glass house, lit up
In the gardens, just the two of you
The brothers, mid-conversation
Same spot, looking at the camera now
Just the two of you, lights overhead
Among the pines, somewhere above the clouds
Proof we showed up (feet first)
A Small Aside

Not every photo needs to be deep

Somewhere between the meaningful moments, someone always points a phone at the ground and says "wait, get our shoes." This is that photo. No lesson here, Papa — just proof that we were all there, standing close, being a little ridiculous together.

Father's Day
Dear Papa,

We've been going through old photos for this — Shimla, Bangalore, that little hill station we can never remember the name of — and noticed something. In almost every one, you're not really looking at the camera. You're looking at us.

That's the thing about you we didn't have words for as kids and have too many words for now: you were never the loudest person in the room, but you were always the steadiest. The one who found the rooftop with the view, who carried us when we were small and still shows up the same way now that we're not.

We don't say thank you nearly enough — for the early mornings, the long drives, the quiet ways you've shown up for thirty-odd years without ever needing it pointed out. So here it is, in writing, on a website you definitely didn't expect: thank you, Papa. For all of it.

Here's to many more rooftops, more pine forests, more "just one more photo." We love you.

With all our love Your Family
Happy Father's Day

Thank you for being our first home, Papa.

tap the heart
Made with love, for the man who made everything possible
